data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Toyota Motor (TSE:7203) After Its Strong Five Year Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Toyota Motor's current share price offers good value or is already pricing in a lot of optimism, this article breaks down what the numbers are actually saying.
  • The stock last closed at ¥3,319, with returns of 2.0% over 7 days, a 4.3% decline over 30 days, a 2.4% decline year to date, and gains of 41.3% over 1 year, 99.9% over 3 years, and 124.1% over 5 years.
  • Investors have been watching Toyota closely as it continues to feature in global discussions around vehicle electrification, hybrid leadership, and the role of traditional automakers in the shift toward lower emission transportation. At the same time, headlines around supply chain resilience, capital investment in new technologies, and competitive pressure from pure play EV makers help frame how the market is currently thinking about the stock.
  • Toyota scores 4 out of 6 on Simply Wall St's valuation checks, giving it a valuation score of 4. The rest of this article will unpack what different valuation methods say about that number and will point to an even more complete way to think about valuation at the end.

Toyota Motor delivered 41.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Auto industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Toyota Motor Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today using a required rate of return.

For Toyota Motor, the 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model starts from last twelve month free cash flow of ¥758.4b and uses analyst and extrapolated forecasts to build a 10 year path. Analyst inputs cover the earlier years, and Simply Wall St extends these with its own estimates so that projected free cash flow reaches ¥3,089.9b by 2030.

Those yearly cash flows, all in ¥, are then discounted and summed to arrive at an intrinsic value estimate of ¥2,214.65 per share. Compared with the recent share price of ¥3,319, the model points to the stock trading at a premium, with the DCF indicating it is 49.9% overvalued.

This does not mean the market is wrong. However, it does suggest that at the current price investors are paying well above what this particular cash flow model supports.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Toyota Motor may be overvalued by 49.9%. Discover 19 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Toyota Motor.

Approach 2: Toyota Motor Price vs Earnings (P/E)

For a profitable company like Toyota, the P/E ratio is a useful way to gauge what you are paying for each unit of current earnings. It is a common anchor for comparing valuation across the Auto industry.

In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk tend to support a higher P/E as investors are willing to pay more for each unit of earnings. Lower growth and higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E. That context matters when you compare ratios rather than just looking at the headline number.

Toyota currently trades on a P/E of 11.71x. That sits slightly below the peer average of 11.97x and well below the Auto industry average P/E of 18.36x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio for Toyota of 19.24x, which is the P/E that would be expected given factors such as its earnings profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for company specific drivers instead of treating all automakers as identical. Set against the current 11.71x, the 19.24x Fair Ratio points to Toyota trading below that customised benchmark.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 11 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Toyota Motor Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so Narratives bring your view on Toyota Motor together into a simple story that links its business direction, expected revenue, earnings and margins to a forecast and a Fair Value you can compare with today’s share price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives give you a clear bridge from story to numbers to action. They help you decide whether the Fair Value you believe in is above or below the current ¥3,319 price, and they update automatically as new news or earnings arrive.

For Toyota, one investor might lean toward the more optimistic scenario with a Fair Value around ¥3,900 and assumptions like revenue growth of 5.9% a year and earnings of ¥4,666.3b by 2028. Another might align with the cautious view with Fair Value near ¥2,651.4, revenue growth of 1.9% a year and earnings of ¥3,298.8b. Narratives let you see exactly which story you are backing.

For Toyota Motor however, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Toyota Motor Narratives:

🐂 Toyota Motor Bull Case

Fair value: ¥3,992.32

Implied discount to this fair value: around 17.0% below the narrative estimate based on the current ¥3,319 price.

Revenue growth assumption: 3.6% a year.

  • Analysts in this camp see steady revenue growth and slightly higher profit margins over the next few years, with earnings reaching ¥4,270.3b by around 2029.
  • The view leans on internal battery production, higher value chain earnings, and operational efficiency to support earnings and cash flows.
  • The narrative also flags risks around production halts, currency moves and competition, and encourages you to stress test the assumptions before relying on the ¥3,992.32 fair value.

🐻 Toyota Motor Bear Case

Fair value: ¥2,651.40

Implied premium to this fair value: around 25.2% above the narrative estimate based on the current ¥3,319 price.

Revenue growth assumption: 1.9% a year.

  • This view is based on the more cautious analyst targets, with revenue growth of 1.9% a year and earnings easing to ¥3,298.8b by about 2028.
  • It focuses on pressures such as a faster shift toward battery electric vehicles, higher costs from tariffs and localization, and heavier spending on batteries and software defined vehicles.
  • The bear narrative also recognises that brand strength, hybrids and value chain income could offset some of these pressures, which is why it encourages you to compare its ¥2,651.4 fair value with your own expectations for demand and margins.

data.title: Compañía Electro Metalúrgica S.A. (SNSE:ELECMETAL) Looks Interesting, And It's About To Pay A Dividend
data.description:
Compañía Electro Metalúrgica S.A. (SNSE:ELECMETAL) stock is about to trade ex-dividend in 3 days. The ex-dividend date is two business days before a company's record date in most cases, which is the date on which the company determines which shareholders are entitled to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is important as the process of settlement involves at least two full business days. So if you miss that date, you would not show up on the company's books on the record date. Therefore, if you purchase Compañía Electro Metalúrgica's shares on or after the 16th of April, you won't be eligible to receive the dividend, when it is paid on the 21st of April.

The company's upcoming dividend is CL$323.00 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of CL$607 per share to shareholders. Looking at the last 12 months of distributions, Compañía Electro Metalúrgica has a trailing yield of approximately 4.1% on its current stock price of CL$14930.00. If you buy this business for its dividend, you should have an idea of whether Compañía Electro Metalúrgica's dividend is reliable and sustainable. So we need to check whether the dividend payments are covered, and if earnings are growing.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Dividends are typically paid out of company income, so if a company pays out more than it earned, its dividend is usually at a higher risk of being cut. Compañía Electro Metalúrgica paid out a comfortable 36% of its profit last year. Yet cash flow is typically more important than profit for assessing dividend sustainability, so we should always check if the company generated enough cash to afford its dividend. It paid out more than half (52%) of its free cash flow in the past year, which is within an average range for most companies.

It's encouraging to see that the dividend is covered by both profit and cash flow. This generally suggests the dividend is sustainable, as long as earnings don't drop precipitously.

View our latest analysis for Compañía Electro Metalúrgica

Click here to see how much of its profit Compañía Electro Metalúrgica paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Stocks in companies that generate sustainable earnings growth often make the best dividend prospects, as it is easier to lift the dividend when earnings are rising. If earnings decline and the company is forced to cut its dividend, investors could watch the value of their investment go up in smoke. That's why it's comforting to see Compañía Electro Metalúrgica's earnings have been skyrocketing, up 22% per annum for the past five years.

Many investors will assess a company's dividend performance by evaluating how much the dividend payments have changed over time. Compañía Electro Metalúrgica has delivered 1.7% dividend growth per year on average over the past 10 years. Earnings per share have been growing much quicker than dividends, potentially because Compañía Electro Metalúrgica is keeping back more of its profits to grow the business.

Final Takeaway

Is Compañía Electro Metalúrgica an attractive dividend stock, or better left on the shelf? From a dividend perspective, we're encouraged to see that earnings per share have been growing, the company is paying out less than half of its earnings, and a bit over half its free cash flow. Overall we think this is an attractive combination and worthy of further research.

So while Compañía Electro Metalúrgica looks good from a dividend perspective, it's always worthwhile being up to date with the risks involved in this stock. For instance, we've identified 3 warning signs for Compañía Electro Metalúrgica (2 are a bit unpleasant) you should be aware of.
